home.eyebrow: A guide to Aracaju and São Cristóvão
home.titulo1: Aracaju's coastline and the hills of
home.subtitulo: Real Google ratings, reviews from people who went, and the way to each place: bus, car or Uber. No need to open ten sites.
home.previewTexto: Sergipe is Brazil's smallest state by area (21,938 km², IBGE). The capital, Aracaju, covers 182.16 km² and has about 630,000 residents (IBGE 2025 estimate). It was founded on paper in 1855 — engineer Pirro's chessboard grid — when the capital moved from São Cristóvão.
home.previewFonte: Sources: IBGE Cities and States · Population estimate 1 Jul 2025
home.topSubtitulo: Sorted by the real Google rating. Tied? The one with more reviews wins.
home.faixaOnibusTexto: Fares are paid only with the Mais Aracaju card, tapped on the reader. The card itself is free and sold at any terminal. We explain where to buy it, how to top it up, and when Uber is the cheaper call.
home.saoCristovaoTexto: São Cristóvão was Sergipe's capital for 265 years and is the fourth-oldest town in the country. São Francisco Square is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. You can do the round trip from Aracaju in half a day.

transporte.metaTitulo: Getting around: bus, car or Uber
transporte.metaDescricao: How to buy the Mais Aracaju card (buses don't take cash), useful bus lines for tourists, and when a car or Uber makes more sense.
transporte.subtitulo: Bus, car or Uber? Start with the thing that catches most tourists off guard:
transporte.alertaTitulo: Buses in Aracaju don't take cash
transporte.alertaTexto: The R$ 4.50 fare is paid only with the Mais Aracaju card, tapped on the reader. The prepaid card itself is free; you only pay the first top-up.
transporte.cartaoTitulo: The Mais Aracaju card in 3 steps
transporte.passo1Texto: Ask for the "Mais Aracaju avulso" card at any integration terminal (Atalaia, DIA, Mercado, Campus or Rodoviária), at the CEAC on Rua do Turista, or from the vest-wearing street agents around the city. The card is free; you only pay the initial top-up (minimum R$ 4.50, one fare).
transporte.passo2Texto: In person: terminal kiosks, street agents and Piu-network pharmacies, paying with cash, Pix or card. Online: the RecargaPay app or the WhatsApp assistant Val, +55 79 3045-2550. Online top-ups must be validated by tapping the card on a bus or terminal reader.
transporte.passo3Texto: On the bus, tap the card on the reader at the entrance. The display shows your balance, deducts R$ 4.50 and releases the turnstile. Inside integration terminals you can switch lines without paying again.
transporte.maisInfo: More information and online top-up on the official site:
transporte.linhasSubtitulo: Flat fare of R$ 4.50. Switching buses inside integration terminals costs nothing extra.
transporte.appsTexto: Live schedules and routes: get CittaMobi (the official app used in Aracaju) or Moovit.
transporte.escolhaSubtitulo: Aracaju is flat, traffic is light outside rush hour and waterfront parking is free. The answer depends on your itinerary:
transporte.carro1: You want the Sunset Waterfront, the southern beaches or São Cristóvão: 20 to 35 km drives with easy parking.
transporte.carro2: You're planning day trips (Xingó Canyon, Mangue Seco).
transporte.carro3: You're with family or a group: the cost per person drops a lot.
transporte.carro4: Rentals start around R$ 90–130 a day plus fuel. Waterfront parking is free.
transporte.uber1: You'll stay between the Atalaia waterfront and Downtown: short rides, R$ 18–25.
transporte.uber2: You're having dinner at Passarela do Caranguejo or going out at night, with no parking or drinking worries.
transporte.uber3: You want to visit Downtown on a weekday (parking there is hard).
transporte.uber4: You're traveling alone or as a couple for a few days: cheaper than rental plus fuel.
transporte.contaTexto: A full day between the waterfront and Downtown runs about R$ 50–70 in Ubers, cheaper than renting. A day with the Sunset Waterfront and southern beaches tops R$ 150 in rides; that day, the car wins. Many people split it: Uber in town, rental car on beach and day-trip days.
transporte.ctaTitulo: Every place has its own recommendation
transporte.ctaTexto: On each attraction's page we show the bus lines that serve it, the Uber estimate and whether driving is worth it.

historia.metaDescricao: The planned capital of 1855 and Brazil's fourth-oldest town, founded in 1590. Timeline and practical facts.
historia.subtitulo: Sergipe has had two capitals. The old one, São Cristóvão, was born in 1590. The new one, Aracaju, was drawn on paper and became capital in 1855. The distance between them: 25 km.
historia.aracajuResumo: Aracaju didn't grow slowly like most Brazilian cities: it was designed to be a capital. On March 17, 1855, provincial president Inácio Barbosa moved the capital from São Cristóvão to the small settlement of Santo Antônio do Aracaju, chasing a port deep enough to ship sugar. Engineer Sebastião Basílio Pirro drew the streets as a 32-block checkerboard, a grid Downtown still keeps. The name comes from Tupi; the most accepted reading joins ará (macaw) and acayú (cashew).
historia.saoCristovaoResumo: Cristóvão de Barros founded São Cristóvão in 1590, at the river mouth, during the conquest of Sergipe. After two attacks and one relocation, the town settled on the hill where it stands today. It was Sergipe's capital for 265 years, until it lost the title to Aracaju in 1855, and then froze in time, which saved its colonial core. In 2010, São Francisco Square joined the UNESCO World Heritage list.
historia.fatoClimaTexto: Warm all year, 26–31 °C. It rains more from April to August.
historia.fatoMelhorEpocaTexto: September to March: steady sun and calm sea. June brings forró and the June festivals.
historia.fatoPratosTexto: Crab, stingray moqueca, aratu, beiju and cashew nuts. In São Cristóvão, queijadinha and convent sweets.

eventos.1590: Cristóvão de Barros founds São Cristóvão at the mouth of the Sergipe river, Brazil's fourth town.
eventos.1607: The town moves to the top of the hill, where it remains today.
eventos.1637: Dutch invasion: the town is looted and partly burned.
eventos.1746: The Rosário dos Homens Pretos church is built by Black brotherhoods.
eventos.1855-1: March 17: Inácio Barbosa moves the capital to Aracaju. São Cristóvão declines, and that is what preserves its old houses.
eventos.1855-2: Engineer Pirro draws Aracaju as a checkerboard: 32 blocks of 110 × 110 meters.
eventos.1908: Electricity arrives; Aracaju grows beyond Pirro's square.
eventos.1926: São Cristóvão gets Brazil's first Christ the Redeemer monument, on top of the hill.
eventos.1967: São Cristóvão's historic center is listed by IPHAN.
eventos.1993: Aracaju opens the Atalaia waterfront Arches, its modern postcard.
eventos.2010: São Francisco Square becomes a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
eventos.2025: The Sergipe History Museum reopens after five years of restoration.
